```json
{
  "risk": "MEDIUM",
  "riskReason": "New caching mechanism for permission denials introduces potential logical inconsistency between denial cache and permission cache, risking incorrect authorization decisions.",
  "circularTests": {
    "detected": false,
    "count": 0,
    "total": 0,
    "details": ""
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Logic Bug",
      "file": "pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go",
      "line": 116,
      "title": "Permission denial cache checked before permission cache lookup",
      "description": "The `permDenialCache` is checked and returns early (lines 116-120) BEFORE attempting to retrieve cached permissions (line 122). This creates a race condition: if a permission denial was cached, subsequent calls will always deny even if the underlying permission state changes or if the permission cache has a valid entry. The denial cache should only be used as a secondary check after confirming the permission cache is stale.",
      "suggestion": "Reorganize logic to check the permission cache first (getCachedIdentityPermissions), and only consult the denial cache if the permission cache is empty/expired. Alternatively, ensure denial cache entries have the same TTL as permission cache entries and consider them together in a single cache lookup."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Incomplete Implementation",
      "file": "pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go",
      "line": 153,
      "title": "Permission denial cache populated without clearing on state changes",
      "description": "The code sets `permDenialCache` when access is denied (line 154), but there is no mechanism shown to invalidate this cache when user permissions are updated, roles are changed, or resources are modified. This can cause stale denials to persist for the cache TTL even after the user gains permission.",
      "suggestion": "Implement cache invalidation hooks: when user permissions, roles, or resource ownership changes, clear the corresponding denial cache entries along with permission cache entries."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Type Safety",
      "file": "pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go",
      "line": 154,
      "title": "Incorrect type stored in permDenialCache",
      "description": "The cache is defined as `*cacheWrap[bool]` but `s.permDenialCache.Set(ctx, permDenialKey, true)` passes a `bool` value. However, looking at the cache key construction and usage pattern, storing `true` as a sentinel has no semantic meaning—the presence/absence of a cache entry is what matters. This invites future confusion and potential bugs if code tries to check the cached bool value.",
      "suggestion": "Either store an empty struct `struct{}{}` to indicate presence-only semantics, or refactor to use a `map[string]struct{}` or similar presence-based structure that better matches the usage pattern."
    }
  ]
}
```
